key_insight: A D-gun applied Al2O3-TiO2 ceramic coating significantly improves the wear resistance of Ti-6Al-4V, and random forest best predicts wear from test conditions.
engineering_relevance: This suggests detonation-sprayed ceramic coatings can extend the tribological life of titanium alloy components in aerospace, biomedical, and marine service.
method: Applied ~200 µm Al2O3-TiO2 coating to Ti-6Al-4V using detonation gun spraying.
Ran ASTM G99 pin-on-disc wear tests at 40–100 N, 100–200 rpm, and 20–40 min against EN31 steel.
Built ANN, KNN, and random forest models to predict wear from sliding parameters.
evidence_signal: SEM showed reduced wear and formation of a protective tribo-layer on coated surfaces, and random forest gave the lowest mean percentage and mean squared error among the tested models.
watch_item: The reported ML performance is based on a limited experimental parameter range, so broader validation is needed before using the model for wider operating conditions.
paper_title: Tribological enhancement of Al 2 O 3 -TiO 2 D-gun coated Ti-6Al-4V alloy: A machine learning approach
